titleStmt.title: SII 28.245: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 245. (A.R. No. 400 of 1913.) TOḺUDŪR, VRIDDHACHALAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Records that Chembaiyāḷi-alias Chēdārāya-veḷḷāṉ, (also named Chembai-dēvaṉ alias Nāyaka-vēḷān in the sequel), gifted the images for procession purposes, of God Tirumēṉi-nāyakar, Goddess Nāchchiyār, the God's consort, in the temple of Uḍaiyār Madhurāntakēśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. He also set up the deities Tirukkūttum-āḷuḍaiyār and consort and constructed in stone the structure of the temple and added a pavilion (tirumaṇṭapam). The inscription ends abruptly.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum
pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ karuvūruṅ koṇṭaruḷi
ya tiripuvaṉaviratevarkku yāṇṭu 3 10 2 vaṭakarai Irā
ciṅka vaḷanāṭṭu Ukaḷūrkkūṟṟattu perun toḻuvūr
Uṭaiyār maturāntakicuvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉārt tiru
nāḷ Eḻuntaruḷḷa tirumeṉināyakaraiyum nācciyāraiyum Uṭai
yārkku tampirāṭṭiyāraiyum Eḻuntaruḷuvitta cempai
yāḷiyāṉa cetārāyaveḷḷāṉ tirukkūttum Āḷḷu
ṭaiyāraiyum nācciyāraiyum Eḻuntaruḷivittā cema
pai tevaṉāna nāyakaveḷāṉ tirukaṟṟaḷiyum tirumaṇṭa
pamu ceyvitta
Digital edition of SII 28.245 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
